name: Oxybutynin
risk: Cognitive decline, delirium, falls, constipation, urinary retention. Opposes donepezil.

name: Diphenhydramine
risk: Sedation, delirium, falls, anticholinergic toxicity

name: Amitriptyline
risk: Strong anticholinergic, cardiac conduction, orthostatic hypotension, sedation

name: Chlorpromazine
risk: High anticholinergic, sedation, orthostatic, EPS

name: Paroxetine
risk: Most anticholinergic SSRI. Hyponatremia, drug interactions (CYP2D6)

name: Olanzapine
risk: STRONGEST anticholinergic among common antipsychotics. Weight gain, metabolic syndrome

name: Diazepam
risk: Long-acting. Falls, sedation, cognitive impairment, paradoxical agitation

name: Zolpidem
risk: Falls, confusion, complex sleep behaviors, fractures

name: Temazepam
risk: Falls, sedation, next-day impairment

name: Glyburide
risk: Prolonged hypoglycemia (long half-life, active metabolites). Use glipizide instead

name: Megestrol
risk: Thrombotic events, minimal appetite benefit, fluid retention, adrenal suppression

name: Doxazosin
risk: Orthostatic hypotension → falls. Not first-line for HTN

name: Metoclopramide
risk: EPS, tardive dyskinesia (risk increases with duration). Max 12 weeks

name: Sliding-scale insulin
risk: Reactive dosing without proactive management. Hypoglycemia risk

name: Meperidine
risk: Neurotoxic metabolite (normeperidine) → seizures. NEVER use in elderly

name: Donepezil
risk: GI side effects (take with food, NOT on empty stomach). Does NOT prevent postop delirium

name: Haloperidol
risk: EPS, QTc prolongation. Lower anticholinergic than olanzapine

name: Quetiapine
risk: Sedation, orthostatic, metabolic. Moderate anticholinergic

name: Risperidone
risk: EPS at higher doses, orthostatic. Low anticholinergic

name: Metformin
risk: CI if CrCl<30. Lactic acidosis rare. Causes anorexia/weight loss in thin elderly. Hold 48h pre-op

name: Alendronate
risk: Esophagitis (take upright with water). CI in reflux esophagitis → use denosumab

name: Apixaban
risk: Safest DOAC in CKD. CI with mechanical valves. Dose reduce if ≥2: age≥80, weight≤60kg, Cr≥1.5

name: Sertraline
risk: Hyponatremia risk (like all SSRIs). Generally safe in elderly. Low anticholinergic

name: Bupropion
risk: LOWEST hyponatremia risk among antidepressants. Seizure risk at high doses. Activating

name: Mirtazapine
risk: Sedation (good if insomnia), weight gain (good if anorexic). Monitor metabolic

name: Nitrofurantoin
risk: Avoid if CrCl<30: pulmonary toxicity, peripheral neuropathy, hepatotoxicity

name: Nifedipine IR
risk: Reflex tachycardia, hypotension, risk of CV events. Use long-acting formulations instead

name: Cyclobenzaprine
risk: Anticholinergic, sedation, falls, ineffective for spasticity in elderly. Avoid >2 weeks

name: Methocarbamol
risk: Sedation, dizziness, falls. Limited efficacy. Avoid in elderly

name: Promethazine
risk: Strong anticholinergic, sedation, EPS, falls, delirium. Avoid in elderly

name: Clonidine
risk: CNS depression, rebound hypertension, bradycardia. High risk of adverse CNS effects in elderly. Avoid as first-line antihypertensive.

name: Digoxin
risk: Narrow therapeutic index. Avoid doses >0.125mg/day in elderly. Toxicity risk with renal impairment, hypokalemia. Monitor levels.

name: Ketorolac
risk: High GI bleeding risk, AKI. Avoid in elderly especially >5 days. Beers: avoid all non-COX-selective NSAIDs chronically.

name: Hydroxyzine
risk: Strong anticholinergic. Sedation, confusion, urinary retention, dry mouth. ACB=3. Use alternatives for anxiety/pruritus.

name: Chlorpropamide
risk: Prolonged hypoglycemia (long half-life 36h). SIADH/hyponatremia. Beers: avoid in all older adults. Use short-acting agents.

name: Prazosin
risk: Orthostatic hypotension, syncope, falls. Beers: avoid as antihypertensive. High first-dose syncope risk.

name: Amiodarone
risk: Thyroid toxicity, pulmonary fibrosis, hepatotoxicity, QT prolongation. Beers: avoid as first-line for AF. Multiple drug interactions.

name: Carisoprodol
risk: Sedation, dependence potential, anticholinergic effects. Metabolized to meprobamate (anxiolytic). Beers: avoid.

name: Trimethobenzamide
risk: Extrapyramidal effects, limited efficacy. Beers: avoid. One of least effective antiemetics.

name: Ibuprofen
risk: GI bleeding (3-4x risk in elderly), AKI, HTN, HF exacerbation. Avoid with anticoagulants, CKD, HF. Max 5 days if used.

name: Ferrous Sulfate >325mg
risk: Constipation, nausea. Doses >325mg/day do not increase absorption but increase side effects. Beers: avoid high doses.

name: Nitrazepam
risk: Long-acting benzo. Falls, fractures, cognitive impairment, delirium. Beers: avoid all benzos in elderly.

name: Chlordiazepoxide
risk: Long half-life (24-48h). Accumulation in elderly, prolonged sedation, falls. Beers: avoid.

name: Trihexyphenidyl
risk: Strong anticholinergic for Parkinson's. Confusion, hallucinations, urinary retention, constipation. ACB=3. Avoid in elderly.

name: Benztropine
risk: Anticholinergic for EPS. Cognitive impairment, urinary retention, constipation. ACB=3. Beers: avoid. Use lower risk alternatives.

name: Desipramine
risk: Less anticholinergic than amitriptyline but still risky: orthostatic hypotension, cardiac conduction delays. Beers: avoid.

name: Nortriptyline
risk: Secondary amine TCA — less anticholinergic but still: orthostatic hypotension, QT prolongation. Beers: avoid. SNRI preferred.

name: Insulin (sliding scale only)
risk: Sliding scale alone = poor glycemic control + hypoglycemia risk. Beers: avoid without basal insulin. A1c targets should be relaxed in frail elderly.

name: Propoxyphene
risk: Withdrawn in many countries. Cardiac toxicity, seizures, minimal analgesic benefit. Beers: avoid. Use paracetamol or low-dose opioids instead.

name: Pentazocine
risk: Mixed agonist-antagonist. CNS effects including confusion, hallucinations. Beers: avoid.

name: Ticlopidine
risk: Safer alternatives exist (clopidogrel). Neutropenia, TTP risk. Beers: avoid.

name: Tolterodine
risk: Anticholinergic for OAB. Dry mouth, constipation, confusion in elderly. ACB=3. Consider mirabegron as alternative.

name: Solifenacin
risk: Antimuscarinic for OAB. Cognitive effects, constipation, dry mouth. ACB=3. Beers: avoid if possible.

name: Lisinopril
risk: Monitor K, Cr. Dry cough 10-15%. Hold if SBP<90 or AKI.

name: Ramipril
risk: First-line in HF and post-MI. Monitor K, Cr. Cough common.

name: Enalapril
risk: Monitor K, Cr. Use ramipril/lisinopril if QID dosing impractical.

name: Losartan
risk: Alternative to ACEi if cough. Monitor K, Cr. Uricosuric effect.

name: Valsartan
risk: Monitor K, Cr. Common in HF combo (valsartan/sacubitril).

name: Candesartan
risk: Evidence in HF. Monitor K, Cr.

name: Furosemide
risk: Electrolyte wasting (K, Mg, Na). Ototoxicity at high IV doses. Titrate carefully to prevent AKI/orthostasis.

name: Hydrochlorothiazide
risk: Hyponatremia in elderly (esp women), hypokalemia, hyperuricemia. Rash/photosensitivity.

name: Spironolactone
risk: Hyperkalemia (esp with ACEi/ARB, CKD). Gynecomastia. Monitor K weekly at start.

name: Carvedilol
risk: First-line HFrEF. Non-selective + alpha block → orthostasis. Start low, uptitrate.

name: Metoprolol
risk: Cardioselective. Metoprolol succinate for HF. Bradycardia, fatigue.

name: Bisoprolol
risk: Cardioselective, once-daily. HF mortality benefit. Bradycardia.

name: Atenolol
risk: Renally cleared — accumulates in CKD. Less preferred in elderly than bisoprolol/metoprolol.

name: Amlodipine
risk: Peripheral edema common. Good for isolated systolic HTN in elderly.

name: Diltiazem
risk: AV block, bradycardia. CYP3A4 inhibitor (↑statin, DOAC levels). Avoid with beta-blocker.

name: Verapamil
risk: Severe constipation, AV block, HF exacerbation. CYP3A4 inhibitor. Avoid in HFrEF.

name: Atorvastatin
risk: CYP3A4 substrate — interactions with diltiazem/verapamil/amiodarone/macrolides. Myopathy risk.

name: Simvastatin
risk: Strong CYP3A4 substrate — myopathy risk with CYP3A4 inhibitors. Max 20mg with amlodipine/amiodarone.

name: Rosuvastatin
risk: Not CYP3A4 — preferred when interactions concern. Dose-reduce in CKD.

name: Aspirin
risk: Primary prevention only if >70-75: avoid (USPSTF). Secondary prevention continues.

name: Clopidogrel
risk: CYP2C19 — omeprazole/esomeprazole ↓activation. Use pantoprazole if PPI needed.

name: Warfarin
risk: Narrow therapeutic index. Many interactions (abx, amiodarone, NSAID). Frequent INR monitoring. DOACs preferred if eligible.

name: Rivaroxaban
risk: Once-daily (AF) / BID (VTE). Take with food. CI if CrCl<15. GI bleed risk higher than apixaban.

name: Dabigatran
risk: Dyspepsia common, GI bleed risk, CI if CrCl<30. Avoid in frail elderly per Beers.

name: Edoxaban
risk: Once-daily. Dose reduce for CrCl 15-50 or low weight. Less evidence in very elderly.

name: Omeprazole
risk: Long-term: ↓Mg, B12, Ca (fracture), C. diff, CAP. CYP2C19 inhibitor — ↓clopidogrel. De-escalate when possible.

name: Pantoprazole
risk: Preferred PPI with clopidogrel (minimal CYP2C19 effect). Same long-term risks as other PPIs.

name: Esomeprazole
risk: Same as omeprazole. Avoid with clopidogrel.

name: Lansoprazole
risk: Long-term: fracture, CAP, C. diff. De-escalate to H2 or prn when possible.

name: Ranitidine
risk: Withdrawn in many countries (NDMA contamination). Confusion/delirium in elderly. Use famotidine.

name: Famotidine
risk: Renally cleared — reduce dose if CrCl<50. Rarely causes delirium at normal doses.

name: Citalopram
risk: Max 20mg/day in elderly (QT prolongation). Hyponatremia risk.

name: Escitalopram
risk: Max 10mg/day in elderly (QT). Hyponatremia risk. Cleaner than citalopram.

name: Fluoxetine
risk: Long half-life (norfluoxetine 1-3 wks) — accumulation in elderly. Many CYP2D6/2C19 interactions.

name: Venlafaxine
risk: BP elevation at high doses. Discontinuation syndrome severe. Hyponatremia.

name: Duloxetine
risk: Used for neuropathic pain. Hepatotoxicity, BP rise, hyponatremia.

name: Trazodone
risk: Commonly used for insomnia in elderly (50mg). Orthostasis, priapism rare, QT at high doses.

name: Lorazepam
risk: Short-intermediate acting. Still Beers-avoid in elderly. Falls, confusion, dependence.

name: Alprazolam
risk: Short half-life → interdose anxiety, dependence. Beers avoid.

name: Clonazepam
risk: Long-acting. Cumulative effects, falls, delirium. Beers avoid.

name: Tramadol
risk: Serotonergic (SS risk with SSRI/SNRI/MAOI). Seizures, hyponatremia. Renal dose reduce.

name: Morphine
risk: Renal metabolite accumulation (M6G) → prolonged effect in CKD. Constipation universal.

name: Oxycodone
risk: Preferred opioid in CKD (no active renal metabolite). Constipation.

name: Fentanyl patch
risk: Only for opioid-TOLERANT patients. Heat/fever increases release → overdose. Not for opioid-naive.

name: Codeine
risk: CYP2D6 variability — some get no analgesia, some toxicity. Constipation.

name: Gabapentin
risk: Renally cleared — dose reduce by CrCl. Sedation, ataxia, falls in elderly. Peripheral edema.

name: Pregabalin
risk: Same class as gabapentin. Renally cleared. Sedation, edema, weight gain, falls.

name: Lamotrigine
risk: SJS risk — titrate slowly. Generally safer than other AEDs in elderly.

name: Valproate
risk: Hepatotoxicity, thrombocytopenia, hyperammonemia. Monitor LFTs, CBC.

name: Levodopa/Carbidopa
risk: Orthostasis, hallucinations, dyskinesia. Protein meals reduce absorption.

name: Rivastigmine
risk: GI side effects. Patch better tolerated than PO. Approved for PDD and AD.

name: Galantamine
risk: GI side effects, bradycardia. CYP2D6/3A4 substrate.

name: Memantine
risk: Moderate-severe AD. Generally well tolerated. Renal dose reduce.

name: Levothyroxine
risk: Absorption reduced by PPI, iron, Ca, soy. Take on empty stomach. Overtreatment → AF, osteoporosis.

name: Tamsulosin
risk: Less orthostasis than doxazosin/terazosin. Floppy iris syndrome (tell ophthalmologist).

name: Finasteride
risk: Slow onset (6 mo). Sexual side effects. Lowers PSA 50%.

name: Mirabegron
risk: Alternative to anticholinergic OAB drugs — preferred in elderly. Small BP rise.

name: Acetaminophen
risk: FIRST-LINE analgesic in elderly. Max 3g/day (not 4g) if frail/malnourished/liver disease.

name: Colchicine
risk: Renal/hepatic dose reduce. Myopathy with statins, neuropathy. CYP3A4/P-gp substrate.

name: Allopurinol
risk: Start low 50mg in CKD. SJS risk (HLA-B*5801 Asian). Interaction with azathioprine.

name: Glipizide
risk: Preferred sulfonylurea in elderly (short half-life, no active metabolites). Still hypoglycemia risk.
